history.title: Our Journey: A Legacy of Service

1985.title: Founding of the Party
1985.desc: The party was founded with a singular goal to champion the rights and aspirations of the Tamil people. Built on principles of social justice and economic equality.

1992.title: First Major Electoral Victory
1992.desc: Building on robust public support, the party achieved its first major electoral victory, securing numerous seats in the state assembly.

2001.title: Launch of Education Reforms Program
2001.desc: Under the leadership of its charismatic leader, a groundbreaking education reform program was introduced to improve access to quality education.

2010.title: Economic Empowerment Initiatives
2010.desc: Focused on sustainable growth and poverty alleviation, several economic empowerment initiatives were launched for small and medium-sized enterprises.

2023.title: Vision 2030: A Progressive Blueprint
2023.desc: Looking towards the future, the Vision 2030 blueprint was unveiled, outlining ambitious plans for technological advancement and sustainable development.

leader.name: Gopal Urangapuli
leader.bio_short: A visionary leader and a dedicated servant of the people, Dr. Bharathi has tirelessly championed the rights and welfare of Tamil Nadu for over two decades. His unwavering commitment to social justice, economic empowerment, and sustainable development has inspired millions.
leader.biography_text: Born in a small village in Madurai district, Dr. Bharathi's journey has been one of profound dedication to public service. From an early age, he exhibited a keen intellect and a deep empathy for the struggles of the common people.
leader.early_life_text: Growing up amidst the rich cultural heritage and socio-political fervor of Tamil Nadu, Dr. Bharathi was profoundly influenced by the ideals of Dravidian movement and social equality. His academic parents focused on sustainable governance and inclusive development.
leader.politics_title: Entry into Politics and Rise to Leadership
leader.politics_text: Dr. Bharathi entered politics with a clear vision to create a prosperous and equitable Tamil Nadu. He quickly gained recognition for his articulate speeches and genuine connection with the electorate.
achievements_list: Spearheaded the 'Clean Cauvery Initiative': A landmark project that revitalized river ecosystems.
Introduced the 'Rural Empowerment Bill': Provided crucial financial support and skills to 50,000 entrepreneurs.
Championed the 'Digital Literacy Drive': Implemented digital education programs in 5,000 schools.
Advocated for increased healthcare funding: Secured state budget allocations for upgrading public hospitals.

The Role of Youth in Governance - University Conclave 2021

manifesto.title: Our Vision, Our Promise: The Manifesto
manifesto.representative_text:
Tamil Nadu Viduthalaip Puli Katchi

Party Policy Leadership:
Under the leadership of Mother Tamil

1. Avvai - Tolkappiyar
2. Mangayarkkarasiyar - Thiruvalluvar
3. Karaikkal Ammaiyar - Vallalar
4. Kannagi - Sankaralinganar

Who is a Tamil?
Only those born in Tamil communities with Tamil as their mother tongue are Tamils. Tamil community identity can be inherited only through the paternal line.

Who is this party for?
• Only those born in Tamil communities can become members.
• Eligibility to lead every level of party structure belongs only to people from Tamil communities.
• Non-Tamil-language groups will not be given any responsibility in the party and cannot become even basic members.

Who can lead the party?
• Under the leadership of a person born in a historically oppressed Tamil community, and with coalition governance under Tamil leadership, Tamil Nadu politics can transform and Tamil historical recovery can happen.
• After the historically oppressed Tamil communities advance in life, and after all Tamil community relations evolve into an equal social family, a qualified person born in a Tamil community will be selected as party leader by administrators across all levels of the party.
• From local body to Parliament, election candidates will be selected by a committee formed with representation from all Tamil communities. In our governance, the Speaker of the Assembly will be a person born in a historically oppressed Tamil community.

What qualification is required for party leadership?
• Commitment to Tamil community unity and a deep desire for ethnic liberation.

What discipline applies to party leadership?
• The party leader will handle only organizational structuring of the party.
• Opportunities are available only as people's representatives (from ward member to legislative assembly and parliament member).
• No position as Chief Minister or Minister.

What is the party's primary policy?
• Elimination of Aryan-Dravidian division politics.
• Social equality for historically oppressed Tamil communities and liberation of the Tamil national identity.
• A united life where all Tamils stand together as Tamil communities.

Guidelines for party events:
• Using representation from 5 Tamil communities, along with state-level coordinators from other Tamil communities, arrangements can be made for election campaigns and all public meetings and protests. Permission must be obtained from the party leader for these events.

Party Rules
Core Philosophy
• This party is not a political structure where power is imposed from top to bottom.
• It functions as a people-centric political movement that rises from the people to the level of governance.

Responsibility Eligibility
• All party responsibilities are not position-based titles.
• They are assigned only on the basis of work, accountability, and field experience.

Discipline and Language Respect
• This party is a movement that conducts politics not through verbal violence, but through ideological firmness and public respect.
• No party functionary or member may use vulgar, obscene, insulting, or abusive words in stage speeches, public meetings, media interactions, or direct public engagement.
• No matter how severe ideological differences are, emotional reactions must be avoided; only ideas must be addressed while preserving language dignity. This is the party's core political discipline.
• This discipline is equally and mandatorily applicable to all leadership levels, from public coordinators to people's architects.

What if discipline is violated?
Any person violating this rule, regardless of their other major contributions to the party,
• must issue a public apology in the name of the party, and
• must then step away from the party.
There are no exceptions.
If there is a complaint that this rule was misused with bad intent, the party will determine truth through inquiry.

Hierarchy of Party Responsibilities
The party structure will function through the following levels:
• State-Level Leadership Architects
• State-Level People's Architects
• District-Level People's Consolidators
• Constituency-Level Public Connectors
• Branch-Level Public Connectors
These 5 levels must operate as interlinked and continuous workflow structures.

Branch-Level Public Connectors
These are field tigers of the party who will build branch units of at least 25-50 people at town/area/village levels.
Duties:
• Engage directly with people on the ground.
• Record social, economic, and political demands of the people.
• Compile information in written, audio, or data format and support constituency-level coordinators for evaluation.

Public Connector (Constituency)
The constituency public connector is the primary field-level officer at constituency scale.
Duties:
• Engage directly with people on the ground.
• Record social, economic, and political demands of the people.
• Compile information in written, audio, or data format.
• Forward collected information to district level.
A public connector has no authority to make policy decisions individually.

People's Consolidator (District)
The district people's consolidator serves as district-level coordinator.
Duties:
• Consolidate information received from constituencies.
• Convert individual issues into district-wide public issues.
• Classify data and set priorities.
• Submit district reports to state level.
A people's consolidator must not alter or suppress information.

People's Architect (State)
The people's architect is a senior state-level responsibility holder.
Duties:
• Review district consolidations.
• Convert people's demands into policy, movement, protest, and election programs.
• Form state-level political decisions with approval from state leadership architects.
• Determine the overall political direction of the party.
State decisions must be based only on data submitted by people's consolidators.

Promotion and Eligibility
• Only those who served as public connectors can be promoted as people's consolidators.
• Only those who served as people's consolidators can be selected as people's architects.
• Higher responsibilities will not be assigned to those without field experience.

Power Control
• No responsibility holder at any level may make personal decisions against information received from the people.
• Any action violating this structure will be treated as a violation of party rules.

Core Political Flow
Field Contact -> Information Consolidation -> State Structuring
This flow is the party's permanent political practice.

Primary Commitment
This rule framework is created
• to prevent dynastic politics,
• to control money politics, and
• to build genuine people's leadership.
This is not a promise.
This is how our party will function.

Party Slogan
A Tamil Nadu with social balance and social justice!
Restoration of Tamil civilizational faith is our goal!
manifesto.summary_text_1: The TAMILNADU VIDUTHALAIPPULI KATCHI's Manifesto outlines our unwavering commitment to the holistic development and prosperity of Tamil Nadu. Our vision is rooted in social justice, economic empowerment, environmental sustainability, and cultural preservation, ensuring that every citizen has the opportunity to thrive.
manifesto.summary_text_2: We believe in a future where education is accessible to all, healthcare is comprehensive and affordable, and economic opportunities are abundant and equitable. Our policies are designed to build a strong, resilient, and inclusive society that respects diversity and champions the rights of every individual.
manifesto.key_pillars_text: Key pillars of our manifesto include investing heavily in rural infrastructure, promoting advanced agricultural practices, and fostering industrial growth that creates sustainable jobs. We are dedicated to transparent governance, upholding democratic values, and empowering local self-governance bodies to better serve their communities.
manifesto.environment_text: Environmental protection and climate change mitigation are central to our agenda. We pledge to safeguard our natural resources, promote renewable energy, and ensure a healthy living environment for future generations. Join us in building a vibrant Tamil Nadu, where progress is shared by all.

party_structure.title: Party Structure
party_structure.content:
Layered Responsibility Structure:

The party structure works through the following five levels:
• State Leadership-Level Architects
• State-Level People's Architects
• District-Level People's Consolidators
• Constituency-Level Public Connectors
• Branch-Level Public Connectors

These five levels are interlinked and must function as a continuous work-flow.

Branch-Level Public Connectors:
They function as field tigers by building branch units at village, town, or local area level with at least 25-50 people.

Duties:
• Maintain direct field contact with people
• Record social, economic, and political demands
• Compile information in written, audio, or data format and submit it for constituency-level review

Public Connector (Constituency):
The Public Connector is the primary field-level coordinator at constituency level.

Duties:
• Maintain direct field contact with people
• Record social, economic, and political demands
• Compile information in written, audio, or data format
• Forward collected data to district level

A Public Connector has no authority to make independent policy decisions.

People's Consolidator (District):
The People's Consolidator serves as district-level coordinator.

Duties:
• Consolidate information from constituencies
• Convert individual issues into district-level public issues
• Classify data and assign priorities
• Submit district reports to state level

A People's Consolidator must not alter or hide information.

People's Architect (State):
The People's Architect is the higher state-level functionary.

Duties:
• Review district consolidations
• Convert public demands into policy, movement, protest, and election plans
• Form state-level political decisions with approval from state leadership-level architects
• Define the overall political direction of the party

State decisions must be based only on data submitted through the structured process.

party_policies.title: Party Policies
party_policies.content:
Policy Leadership Lineage:

Under the leadership of Mother Tamil:
• Avvai - Tholkappiyar
• Mangayarkkarasiyar - Thiruvalluvar
• Karaikkal Ammaiyar - Vallalar
• Kannagi - Sankaralinganar

Who is Tamil?
Only those born in Tamil communities with Tamil as mother tongue are Tamil. Tamil community identity is inherited through the father-line in this policy framework.

Who can join the party?
• Only those born in Tamil communities can become members.
• Eligibility to lead all party structures belongs only to Tamil communities.
• Non-Tamil language groups will not be assigned party responsibilities and cannot hold even basic membership.

Who should lead the party?
• Political transformation in Tamil Nadu and historical restoration of Tamils must emerge under leadership from historically oppressed Tamil communities, with a Tamil-led federal governance framework.
• After historically oppressed Tamil communities attain social advancement, and after all Tamil communities evolve as equal people, one eligible person born in a Tamil community will be chosen by administrators across all party levels.
• From local body to parliament, electoral candidates will be selected by a committee formed with representation from all Tamil communities. In our governance model, the legislative speaker will be from a historically oppressed Tamil community.

Core qualification for party leadership:
• A deep commitment to unity of Tamil communities and national liberation of the Tamil people

What is the leadership code?
• The party leader will focus only on party structuring responsibilities.
• Opportunity exists only to serve as people's representative (from ward member to MLA to MP).
• The leader cannot become Chief Minister or Minister.

What is the party's primary policy?
• Elimination of Aryan-Dravidian divide politics
• Social equality for historically oppressed Tamil communities and liberation of Tamil national identity
• A unified life where all Tamils live together as Tamil communities

Event discipline:
Party election campaigns, public meetings, and protests can be organized only by Tamil-community-based teams working with state coordinators of other Tamil communities. Permission from the party leader is mandatory.

Party Regulations:

Foundational principle:
• This party is not a top-down power structure.
• It operates as a people-centered political movement that rises from the public to governance level.

Responsibility eligibility:
• All responsibilities are not title-based.
• They are assigned only on work, accountability, and field experience.

Discipline and language dignity:
• This party conducts politics through ideological clarity and public respect, not verbal aggression.
• No office-bearer or member may use vulgar, abusive, insulting, or degrading language in stage speeches, public meetings, media expression, or direct public interaction.
• Even under severe ideological conflict or provocation, members must respond to ideas without losing language discipline.
• This rule applies equally and compulsorily to all leadership levels.

If discipline is violated:
• The person must issue a public apology in the party's name before the people.
• Then the person must step away from the party.
No exceptions.

If a complaint alleges misuse of this rule, the party will conduct an inquiry and determine facts.

Promotion and eligibility:
• Only a serving Public Connector can be elevated to People's Consolidator.
• Only a serving People's Consolidator can be selected as People's Architect.
• Those without field experience cannot receive higher responsibilities.

Authority control:
• No functionary at any level may take personal decisions that override information received from the people.
• Any such action is treated as a violation of party regulations.

Core political flow:
Field Contact -> Information Consolidation -> State Structuring
This flow is the permanent political practice of the party.

Primary commitment:
This regulatory framework is enacted:
• To prevent family politics
• To control money-driven politics
• To build real people's leadership
This is not a promise; this is the operating rule of our party.

Party Slogan:
A Tamil Nadu of social justice with social equality!
Restoration of Tamil spiritual identity and liberation of the Tamil nation are our goals!

about_party.title: About Party
about_party.content:
This movement is built as a people-centered political force rooted in Tamil identity, social justice, and disciplined public leadership.

Core Direction:
• Build social equality across Tamil communities
• Strengthen democratic participation from grassroots to state level
• Protect linguistic, cultural, and political rights

Organizational Character:
• Responsibility is assigned through work and field experience
• Leadership is structured, accountable, and duty-focused
• Public dignity and language discipline are non-negotiable

Commitment:
The party's long-term mission is to build a just, self-respecting, and united Tamil society through organized public action.

state_rights.title: State Rights
state_rights.content:
Tamil Nadu's political, linguistic, cultural, and social rights must be protected.

State autonomy and social advancement of Tamils remain core objectives of the party.

governance_policies.title: Governance Policies
governance_policies.content:
Tamil National Governance Framework:
Annual rotational community leadership:
• Scheduled rotation ensuring representation
• Pardhi, Vanniyar, Vellala, Devendra Kula Vellalas, Konar communities

Community-led governance with community heads:
• Kuravar community representative as head of governance council
• Artisan, potter, blacksmith, physician communities as senior ministers
• Tamil-led minority community representation cabinet

Social justice for language minorities, education, economic development, employment opportunities and security:

Identity restoration:
Mother-tongue based national identity recognition:
• Legal struggle for ethnic identity restoration including Tamils, Telugus, Kannadigas, Malayalees and other language groups
• Citizenship - Indian citizenship
• Nationality - Tamil/Telugu/Kannada - Nationality

Language-based social justice restructuring:
• Current reservation systems inadequate for creating fair social equity. Language-based proportional reservation across Tamils, Telugus, Malayalees, Kannadigas and other language groups with internal community allocation
• Tamil communities recognized as Tamil people. Restoration of ancient Tamil identity. All Tamil communities integrated into restored Tamil identity framework

Self-reliant Tamil Nadu economy:
• Housing infrastructure for homeless: 2-cent housing units on 4-cent land allocation
• Land ceiling policy: Minimum 1-acre land distribution for the landless
• Three-meal daily wage guarantee through livelihood assurance

Green transition and agricultural renewal:
• Solar power infrastructure investment for all villages and households
• Mandatory two trees per household
• Land ceiling enforcement: Maximum 100 acres per family (parents + 5 children)
• Agricultural pump electrification managed by state
• Free provision of all agricultural inputs and materials
• 20 palm and 2 mixed-species trees per-acre mandated planting
• District-level agricultural manufacturing units establishment
• Field bund specification: 2 feet height, 2 feet width minimum
• Complete irrigation infrastructure guarantee for all agricultural lands

Quality education restoration:
• Free books and fee regulation for all students
• Primary and secondary schools: Mandatory Tamil-medium education
• Union and international curricula: Mandatory Tamil textbooks
• All institutions: Tamil, English and other language compulsory subjects (except testing requirements)
• Tamil language and history: Mandatory across all schools and colleges
• State curriculum reform:
 Tamil (grammar, literature & practice exercises)
 English (grammar & practice exercises)
 Mathematics
 Science
 Arts and Physical Education
 History (Tamil and world history)
 Civics and Environmental Studies
• Government schools: Libraries, sports grounds, laboratories, water facilities and sanitation infrastructure guaranteed
• One early childhood education center per village
• Mother-tongue documentation on all school/college certificates

Social order:
• CCTV surveillance of all villages and public spaces with public verification access
• Complete ban on public statuary. Memorial halls restricted to leaders only
• Total legal prohibition on all narcotics and psychoactive substances
• Caste, religion and community-based organizations banned
• Caste and religion-based sports teams prohibited
• Private schools: Dormitory management by state
• Cremation and burial grounds: Public ownership, accessible to all, penalties for violation

Mother and child welfare:
• Pregnant women: Family lifetime insurance coverage worth ₹1 crore
• First child: ₹8,000 monthly for 5 years
• Second child: ₹10,000 monthly for 5 years
• Third+ children: ₹15,000 monthly for 5 years
• Family planning procedures banned
• Female sterilization methods prohibited

Water systems and river connectivity:
• Water body restoration and river-to-water body connectivity
• Lake and pond construction in all villages with fishery development
• Interstate river interlinking following constitutional law frameworks

Public and private employment:
• Fourth child family heads: Guaranteed government employment based on merit
• Government employment: 80% Tamil community, 15% language minority, 5% Tamil-medium minority education graduates
• Private educational institutions: State-appointed teachers
• Educated unemployment support: ₹5,000 monthly with mandatory social service work, housing and food support, until ₹15,000 monthly income reached
• Private sector senior positions: 50% Tamil community quota
• Private sector mid-level/junior positions: 80% Tamil community quota
• Hundred-days employment program: Agriculture and village maintenance work only
• Private sector placement monitoring
• Government projects: Labor implemented under government officer supervision without contractor system

Transport and roads:
• Road discipline enforcement: Month-long driving ban for first three violations, license revocation for additional violations
• Student transportation: 50% fare subsidized buses for all students
• Panchayat special bus service for farmers and self-employed
• State and national highway restaurants: Government fare regulation with enforcement
• Private bus fares: State regulated with online booking mandated

Spiritual and cultural restoration:
• Tamil spiritual identity restoration through legal struggle: Tamils as distinct people beyond religion; not Hindu, Muslim, Christian, Buddhist, Sikh, Jain
• All government, private institutions, offices, schools and Tamil temples: Tamil Mother deity worship at entry mandatory
• All worship places: Tamil-only worship with encouragement for one Tamil prayer at Islamic shrines
• Religious converts: Cultural deity worship restoration support
• Tamil Nadu government religious affairs department: All worship place and financial auditing oversight
• Community deity worship: No government, community or religious interference permitted

Public services:
• Panchayat, town, city level: One police station per administrative division
• High court branch establishment through legal struggle
• All state rights including education: Recovery through democratic mass mobilization like language rights and jallikattu restoration
• District hospitals: Advanced facilities for all citizens
• Infrastructure access: Supreme court branch establishment through public struggle
• Government sanitation workers: Highest wages in public service
• Single-caste villages: Sanitation workers from that community
• Multi-caste villages: Sanitation workers from all communities
• Urban areas: Fair caste-based sanitation worker representation

Financial regulation:
• All financial collectives: Government accounting oversight
• State identity card system: Complete documentation of certificates and property details
• Trust assets: ₹100 crore wealth concentration prohibition
• Trust financial auditing: Government committee oversight

Justice system:
• Civil cases: One-week disposal mandate
• Criminal cases: Six-month disposal mandate
• Caste murders, sexual violence, communal violence: Life imprisonment without bail
• Corruption, narcotics trafficking, human trafficking, public property damage: Same bail-less life imprisonment provision

Healthcare:
• Emergency medical treatment: Free private hospital care for 2 days
• All villages: Primary health center infrastructure
• Traditional Tamil medicine: Complete preservation and public teaching

Labour rights:
• Eight-hour workday
• Women: 3 paid monthly leave days
• Forced labor prohibition
• Workplace disputes: Direct government resolution rooms in each town
• Mobile application for dispute registration

Commerce:
• Tamil business support through government cooperation
• Tamil-only mandatory shop signage (English allowed)

Banking and resources:
• Annual interest lending by state
• Non-payment: Business transfer to new operator, old owner debt waived, new owner annual interest
• Family death: Government assistance provision
• Tamil Nadu minerals: Tamil Nadu exclusive ownership
• Inter-state/international exchange: Barter-based mineral resource sharing
